code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Defective/unsafe products or complete failure within 6 months
    - Multiple failure incidents across purchases
    - Explicit return/disposal recommendations ("don't buy", "junk")
    - Safety hazards override any positive remarks

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Core functionality failures despite secondary benefits
    - Product requires constant workarounds for basic use
    - Regret language ("wish I bought different brand")
    - Poor value proposition ("not worth the price")

    3 Stars (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Equal balance of strengths/weaknesses
    - Requires modifications to achieve basic functionality
    - Context-dependent praise ("good for X but not Y")
    - Replacement of inferior product without enthusiasm

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Explicit star deduction phrasing ("only 4 because...")
    - Single functional criticism impacting non-core features
    - Requires pre-treatment for full functionality (e.g., pre-wash)
    - Positive experience with 1-2 minor drawbacks ("almost perfect")

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Multi-year validation of durability/performance
    - Combines 3+ positive aspects (function, design, value)
    - Non-functional quirks dismissed as irrelevant to core use
    - "Would repurchase" statements despite minor issues
    - Exceeds needs for specialized use cases

    EDGE CASE HANDLING:
    - Final update overrides initial experience (positive→negative)
    - "Still recommend despite..." →4 if functional issue exists
    - "Perfect except [non-functional]" →5 regardless of quirk
    - Requires breaking-in period ≠ product defect
    - Comparative superiority ("better than previous model") →5
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1 (Critical 1→3 Fix):
Review: "UPDATE: Worked 3 years then failed. New version broke in 2 months."
<Reasoning>Multiple failures across purchases indicate systemic quality issues.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2 (5→4 Error Fix):
Review: "The towels are definitely cute and well made. Only 4 stars because initial absorbency requires 2 washes."
<Reasoning>Explicit star deduction for functional requirement despite praise.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 3 (4→5 Error Fix):
Review: "So far this blender is working well. Makes great smoothies despite occasional noise."
<Reasoning>Minor non-functional issue doesn't detract from core performance.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 4 (Borderline 4):
Review: "Love the collapsible design but fabric sags when full. Still great for lightweight storage."
<Reasoning>Functional limitation acknowledged but recommendation maintained.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5 (Long-Term 5):
Review: "EDIT: After 18 months still perfect! Minor delay in auto-mode irrelevant to results."
<Reasoning>Sustained performance confirmation outweighs trivial quirk.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 6 (Material 5):
Review: "Cotton wrinkles but absorbs better than competitors. Worth every wrinkle!"
<Reasoning>Core functionality outweighs inherent material property.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 7 (Comparative 5):
Review: "Upgrade from old model - quieter and more efficient. Worth the premium."
<Reasoning>Explicit comparison showing measurable improvement.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 8 (2-Star Core Failure):
Review: "Looks great but leaks constantly. Can't perform basic function."
<Reasoning>Aesthetic praise overridden by functional failure.</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 9 (3-Star Compromise):
Review: "Does the job with frequent adjustments. Not bad for occasional use."
<Reasoning>Balanced assessment with usability compromises.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 10 (1-Star Hazard):
Review: "Emits toxic fumes when heated. Immediate disposal required!"
<Reasoning>Safety hazard overrides all other considerations.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>
</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (37 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 15
  gold 5 -> 4: 11
  gold 1 -> 2: 3
  gold 4 -> 3: 3
  gold 3 -> 2: 2

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "Not much to say. They work!"
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "Fit perfect into my CHV1210."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.33
  4. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [3, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "There is only one problem for the experienced cook when it comes to this set - you're likely to already have one or more of these tools and possibly Oxo Good Grips at that! That's why I think this set is best suited for someone setting up a kitchen f..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I bought one of those Pyrex sets a few years ago.  You know, the one that came with the blue lids and some of the containers were rectangular --large and small-- while others were round (like these).  Well, turns out that the ones I used the most wer..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "These not only look great, but they are actually usable. I've bought napkins for my place settings in the past that looks so nice, then I realized they were completely nonabsorbent. So while they looked good, they were pretty much worthless as an act..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "Very nice set of skillets - and what a lovely color.  Quite a bargain, and I was glad to find it as I provide my husband his "own" skillets.  He tends to be a "high heat" cook, and so I don't let him use my expensive skillets.  Was nice to find Farbe..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "This is a sharp well made tablecloth.  It's strengths are the vibrant colors, sharp contrast, and solid construction.  The cons are just the inherent need to iron it that comes from the cotton."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "We are both tough on dish towels and use them constantly. Not only do we cook 90% of our meals at home but we also use fresh fruits and veggies with pretty much every meal and that means a lot of washing, drying and carrying things around...why carry..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 37 errors, 14 borderline out of 80 examples."}
